FIG \u2014 Banks.lede: Morgan Stanley wins crypto trust charter approval while cannabis banking reform resurfaces; political headwinds on CFPB nominee and Fed independence.

bullets.text: Morgan Stanley gets OCC conditional approval for digital-asset trust charter with $50M tier-1 capital requirement—clearing a key regulatory hurdle for crypto services.
Cannabis banking reform reintroduced by Sen. Merkley following DEA's medical cannabis rescheduling, creating a regulatory opening for banks to enter the market.
Warren grills CFPB nominee Brian Johnson (ex-Capital One) over potential conflict of interest regarding dropped Capital One lawsuit—confirmation vote looming.
Supreme Court rules 5-4 Trump cannot fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook, affirming Fed independence and blocking removal attempt.

FIG \u2014 Asset & Wealth Management.lede: RIA consolidation accelerates with EP Wealth's $900M deal; Vanguard outsources quant strategies to T. Rowe Price; regulatory tailwinds unlock retirement income advisory revenue; iCapital flags growth slowdown and AI regulatory risk.

bullets.text: EP Wealth acquires Opes Wealth Management for $900M, pairing the deal with a cash incentive program for non-shareholder employees—signaling aggressive capital deployment and talent retention amid RIA M&A wave.
Vanguard taps T. Rowe Price as subadvisor on three active funds after internal quant strategies underperform, a rare outsourcing move despite resulting fee increases.
Regulatory changes are enabling advisors to embed decumulation strategies into retirement accounts, opening a new advisory revenue stream in retirement income management.
iCapital signals slower growth ahead and flags AI as an emerging regulatory risk, forecasting S&P 500 returns of 0–5% in H2 2026—well below prior consensus.

FIG \u2014 Insurance.lede: Climate risk reshaping insurance supply; pricing pressures offset by innovation and capital solutions expansion.

bullets.text: Allianz warns climate change is rendering entire asset classes uninsurable—insurers withdrawing from high-risk geographies as extreme weather losses mount.
Kingstone cuts cat reinsurance costs 15%+ while doubling XoL limit to $500m for 2026/2027, signaling better market terms despite elevated nat cat exposure.
Aon expands institutional capital access by appointing new head of asset-backed finance & securitization—pushing ILS and structured products as alternative risk transfer.
Fitch flags reinsurers must innovate in microinsurance, parametric, and ILS to expand nat cat coverage affordably and sustainably.

FIG \u2014 Specialty Finance.lede: Sovereigns flooding private credit while mainstream finance converges on digital rails—but crypto litigation risk intensifies.

bullets.text: Sovereign wealth funds are aggressively rotating from public equities/bonds into private credit, sustaining capital demand for specialty finance vehicles.
Visa, Mastercard, US Bank, Google, and Coinbase backing Open USD stablecoin signals institutional-grade convergence on digital currency infrastructure.
1,700 UK investors suing Binance for £150M+ damages exposes regulatory and litigation exposure in crypto platforms despite mainstream adoption momentum.
Google Ventures' $30M Nebex round signals fintech is capturing margin at emerging asset-class intersections (space economy infrastructure).

PE & Deals.lede: Mixed LP signals: major pension funds tightening PE discipline on underperformance, while some LPs selectively deploy into distressed software and complex opportunities.

bullets.text: Colorado PERA ($75bn) reconsidering PE allocation after underperforming benchmarks—clearest sign yet of LP capital discipline tightening across the asset class.
Arizona pension fund bucking the trend, temporarily increasing PE allocation to hunt distressed software assets facing valuation compression and debt walls from peak-multiple buys.
BCI tripled 2025 PE outlays under new global PE head Jon Salon, signaling continued appetite for 'complex' opportunities despite broader LP skepticism.

Markets & Macro.lede: Fed signals no rate guidance ahead, while PE eyes pension buyouts and wealth transfer opportunities—but private credit shows cracks.

bullets.text: Fed Chair Warsh explicitly rejected forward guidance, telling markets to price outcomes independently without central bank telegraphing of future rate moves.
KKR entering UK/European pension buyouts, betting on sponsor demand to offload longevity liabilities at attractive yields as generational risk shifts.
$84 trillion wealth transfer creates $60tn opportunity for asset managers, but historical 70% client attrition on generational handoffs poses retention challenge.
Medallia's post-SPAC equity wipeout signals broader private credit stress when venture-stage models hit growth reality—questioning PE's deeper involvement in later-stage funding.

Newsletters.lede: ETF ecosystem surging past $23T while regulators scrutinize complex products; wealth transfer thesis threatened by healthcare costs; yen volatility signals Fed expectations.

bullets.text: Global ETF assets hit record $23T in May with YTD inflows ($739B) already exceeding last year's pace—structural shift into passive continues.
SEC opening comment period on novel ETF structures (leveraged, inverse, derivatives-linked)—regulatory pressure mounting on complexity as innovation outpaces guardrails.
Healthcare-driven wealth depletion risk: longevity costs could materially erode the $84T intergenerational transfer thesis, reshaping estate and advisory strategies.
JPY hits 40-year low on Fed rate-hike bets, signaling market conviction on tightening and destabilizing traditional safe-haven flows.

Career Intel.lede: No stories in this lane today.

bullets.text: AI cheating on math econ at Brown

all.lede: Morgan Stanley clears digital asset trust hurdle; cannabis banking reform resurfaces; RIA consolidation accelerates as Vanguard makes rare outsourcing move.

bullets.text: Morgan Stanley wins OCC conditional approval for digital-asset trust charter with $50M minimum tier-1 capital requirement—a regulatory green light for crypto infrastructure play.
Sen. Merkley reintroduces cannabis banking reform legislation following DEA's medical cannabis rescheduling, creating a regulatory opening for banks to serve the industry.
EP Wealth acquires Opes Wealth Management for $900M and launches cash-based incentive program, signaling aggressive capital deployment and talent wars in RIA consolidation.
Vanguard outsources three active funds to T. Rowe Price as subadvisor after internal quant strategies underperform—a rare move for Vanguard despite willingness to raise fees.
